symbols: yellow Christ on cross — the Crucifixion transposed into a Breton landscape
Breton women praying — the enduring devotion of simple faith
autumn harvest landscape — the death that precedes new life
description: Gauguin's iconic Yellow Christ shows a crucified Christ rendered in bright yellow against an autumnal Breton landscape. Three peasant women in traditional Breton dress kneel at the foot of the cross. The landscape shows golden harvest fields and green trees under a blue sky. The style is boldly simplified with flat color areas and dark outlines.
theological_description: Gauguin's Yellow Christ transplants the Crucifixion from historical Jerusalem into the living devotion of Breton peasant women, declaring that Christ's sacrifice is not a distant event but a present reality for every believing community. The autumnal setting connects to Jesus's teaching: 'Unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit' (JHN.12.24). The cross in the harvest landscape embodies Paul's declaration: 'Far be it from me to boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ' (GAL.6.14). The Breton women's simple faith reflects Jesus's praise: 'I thank you, Father, that you have hidden these things from the wise and revealed them to little children' (MAT.11.25).

scripture_refs.reason: Boasting only in the cross of Christ
The grain of wheat dying to bear fruit, the autumn harvest setting
Hidden from the wise, revealed to the simple — the peasant women's faith
The word of the cross is the power of God
